title: IPEDS: the federal reporting every US college must do
summary: If a US institution takes federal student aid, it must report to IPEDS — every year, on a fixed calendar. Today: what IPEDS is, who runs it, the three collection periods, and how this shapes the reporting features an SIS must ship.

activities.title: What IPEDS is
activities.markdown:
**IPEDS** (Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System) is a set of **interrelated annual surveys** run by the U.S. Department of Education's **NCES** (National Center for Education Statistics). It collects data from **every institution that participates in federal (Title IV) student-aid programs**.

Reporting is **mandatory** for Title IV participants (20 U.S.C. 1094 §487(a)(17); 34 CFR 668.14(b)(19)). The data spans **eight areas** — including **enrollment, completions, finances, and student financial aid** — submitted through **13 survey components across 3 reporting periods: Fall, Winter, and Spring**.

activities.question: Which institutions must complete IPEDS surveys?
options: Those participating in Title IV federal student-aid programs (mandatory)
activities.explanation: Completing IPEDS surveys is mandatory for any institution participating in (or applying for) a Title IV federal student-aid program.

activities.question: IPEDS data is collected across how many reporting periods each year?
activities.explanation: IPEDS uses 13 survey components grouped into three collection periods: Fall, Winter, and Spring.

activities.question: Which agency operates IPEDS?
options: NCES, within the U.S. Department of Education
activities.explanation: IPEDS is run by the National Center for Education Statistics (NCES), part of the U.S. Department of Education.

activities.title: The IPEDS reporting cycle
activities.mermaid:
flowchart TD
  A[Institution keyholder registers] --> B[Fall collection]
  B --> C[Winter collection]
  C --> D[Spring collection]
  D --> E[NCES edits and locks data]
  E --> F[Public via IPEDS Data Center]

steps.title: Register to report
steps.caption: The institution's IPEDS contact prepares to submit.

steps.title: Edit & lock
steps.caption: NCES runs edits and locks submitted data.

steps.title: Public release
steps.caption: Data becomes available through the IPEDS Data Center.

activities.caption: The Fall/Winter/Spring periods are defined by NCES; the register → submit → lock → release framing is the standard collection workflow.

activities.headline: IPEDS 2024-25 Spring provisional data released
activities.summary: NCES released **provisional data from the 2024-25 Spring collection on January 6, 2026**. Provisional releases let researchers and institutions work with the data before the final, fully-edited files are published.

source.title: IPEDS Survey Components (Latest Data Releases) — NCES

items: **IPEDS** = mandatory annual reporting for **Title IV** institutions, run by **NCES**.
**13 components across 3 periods**: Fall, Winter, Spring.
Covers **enrollment, completions, finance, financial aid**, and more.
An SIS must be able to **generate IPEDS submissions from tenant data** on that calendar.

glossary.definition: Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System — NCES's mandatory annual surveys for Title IV institutions.
National Center for Education Statistics, within the U.S. Department of Education; operates IPEDS.
One IPEDS data collection (e.g., Completions, Finance, Fall Enrollment); there are 13 across three periods.
One of the three IPEDS collection windows: Fall, Winter, or Spring.

reflection: Which ManageArk modules feed IPEDS (enrollment, completions, finance, aid)? How would you generate validated IPEDS submissions from schema-per-tenant data on the Fall/Winter/Spring cadence?
